feed.title: Current Watches, Warnings and Advisories for New Zealand issued by MetService
feed.rights: Copyright Meteorological Service of New Zealand Limited, 2026. Licensed under Creative Commons BY 4.0.

entry.title: Road Snowfall Warning
entry.summary: Expect 1 to 3 cm of snow to settle on the road from this afternoon until early tomorrow morning.

author.name: Meteorological Service of New Zealand Limited

entry.title: Road Snowfall Warning
entry.summary: Rain is expected to turn to snow about the road this afternoon until Monday afternoon. 5 to 10 cm may accumulate on the road above 300 metres, but possibly as much as 30 cm near the summit.

author.name: Meteorological Service of New Zealand Limited

entry.title: Road Snowfall Warning
entry.summary: Snow is expected to continue about the road above 400 metres. Expect another 15 to 25 cm near the summit with 2 to 5 cm down to 400 metres.

author.name: Meteorological Service of New Zealand Limited

entry.title: Road Snowfall Warning
entry.summary: Snow showers expected about and eastwards of the summit. 4 to 6 cm may accumulate above 400 metres and 6 to 10 cm near the summit.

author.name: Meteorological Service of New Zealand Limited

entry.title: Heavy Rain Warning - Orange
entry.summary: Expect 150 to 200 mm of rain about the ranges, with possibly 300 mm about the Inland and Seaward Kaikoura Ranges. During the same period 60 to 100 mm is likely nearer the coast. Peak rates of 15 to 25 mm/h expected Monday night and Tuesday morning about the ranges. Freezing level about 1800 metres and snow likely above 1400 metres. Impact: Dangerous river conditions, flooding, and slips likely. Dangerous driving conditions and road closures are expected. Chance of upgrading to a Red Warning: High. Changes: Event end time extended. Chance of upgrade increased to high.

author.name: Meteorological Service of New Zealand Limited

entry.title: Heavy Rain Watch
entry.summary: Periods of heavy rain, amounts may approach warning criteria. Note this rain falling as snow above 1000 metres, likely reaching as low as 800 metres. Chance of upgrading to a Warning: Moderate.

author.name: Meteorological Service of New Zealand Limited

entry.title: Strong Wind Watch
entry.summary: Southeasterly winds may approach severe gale about exposed valleys. Chance of upgrading to a Warning: Moderate. Changes: Period extended.

author.name: Meteorological Service of New Zealand Limited

entry.title: Strong Wind Watch
entry.summary: Southeast winds may approach severe gale in exposed places. Chance of upgrading to a Warning: Moderate.

author.name: Meteorological Service of New Zealand Limited

entry.title: Heavy Snow Watch
entry.summary: Periods of heavy snow above 800 metres, where snowfall amounts may approach warning criteria. Lesser amounts of snow down to 400 metres. Chance of upgrading to a Warning: Moderate. Changes: Event timing changed.

author.name: Meteorological Service of New Zealand Limited

entry.title: Heavy Rain Warning - Orange
entry.summary: Expect a further 70 to 100 mm of rain. Intensities of 10 to 15 mm/h are possible until early Monday morning. Snow is likely above 300 metres. Impact: Dangerous river conditions, flooding, and slips likely. Dangerous driving conditions and road closures are expected. Chance of upgrading to a Red Warning: High. Changes: Finish time extended to Monday afternoon.

author.name: Meteorological Service of New Zealand Limited

entry.title: Heavy Rain Warning - Red
entry.summary: A further 50 to 70 mm of rain on top of what has already fallen, especially from Milton northwards. Intensities are now generally decreasing, but some locations may still experience 10 to 15 mm/h until early Monday morning. Snow is likely above 300 metres. This is a significant amount of rain for low lying areas, especially around low lying parts of Dunedin and the Taieri Plain. Impact: Threat to life from dangerous river conditions, significant flooding and slips. Conditions will disrupt travel, make some roads impassable, and isolate communities.

author.name: Meteorological Service of New Zealand Limited

entry.title: Heavy Snow Warning - Orange
entry.summary: Further snow is expected during the night above 200 to 300 metres. Between 400 and 600 metres, another 5 to 10 cm of snow is expected, especially south of Queenstown to Middlemarch. Above 600 metres, there could be 20 to 40 cm of snow still to fall. Impact: Travel disruption and damage to trees and powerlines possible. Cold conditions may cause stress for livestock. Chance of upgrading to a Red Warning: Moderate.

author.name: Meteorological Service of New Zealand Limited
